route_ordering_bug.issue: GET /api/warehouse/transfers was returning 404 because it matched /{warehouse_id} pattern first
route_ordering_bug.fix: Moved /transfers route definition before /{warehouse_id} route to ensure proper route matching

stock_reorder_engine.sales_velocity: Calculates average daily sales over configurable period
stock_reorder_engine.po_draft_generation: Groups items by supplier for PO drafts

critical_code_review_comments: Route ordering was critical bug - moved specific routes before parameterized routes
All endpoints properly authenticated and RBAC protected
Audit trails implemented for warehouse and stock settings changes
Stock validation works correctly for transfers (insufficient stock check)
Frontend has all data-testid attributes for testing

success_rate.backend: 96% - 22/23 tests passed (1 skipped - no pending transfers for action test)

seed_data_existing.critical_product: XL Unlimited 30 Hari (PRD002) - current_stock: 0, min: 15, max: 150
seed_data_existing.warehouses: 10 warehouses including Gudang Banjarmasin, Gudang Martapura, Gudang Banjarbaru

context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 3 Stock Reorder Engine & Warehouse Control fully tested. Route ordering bug fixed in warehouse_control.py. Critical product XL Unlimited 30 Hari needs reorder (suggested 150 pcs). 10 warehouses exist. No transfers created yet. API endpoints all working. Frontend pages at /stock-reorder and /warehouse-control.

rca_of_fixed_issue.root_cause: In FastAPI, route patterns are matched in order of definition. The parameterized route /{warehouse_id} was defined before /transfers, so 'transfers' was being treated as a warehouse_id value and looked up in database, returning 404
rca_of_fixed_issue.fix: Moved /transfers route definition above /{warehouse_id} route in warehouse_control.py. Specific routes must always be defined before parameterized routes in FastAPI
rca_of_fixed_issue.verification: After fix, GET /api/warehouse/transfers now returns {items: [], total: 0} correctly
